brief_summary: Force-travel line chart of a single U4T downstroke from 0mm to 4.0mm. The line rises gently, climbs steeply into a flat, coral-highlighted plateau between roughly 1.0mm and 1.8mm of travel labelled '62g actuation, pronounced plateau', then drops and runs flat for the remainder of the stroke to a marked bottom-out at 4.0mm labelled '68g'. A dashed reference line names the 6-gram gap between the plateau's resolution and the bottom-out weight.
data_sources: gazzew-boba-u4t-deep-dive.mdx § 'The bump: why it sits where it does' — 'a D-shaped bump ... a flat plateau across the top rather than a sharp peak' and 'Gazzew positions that plateau high in the 4.0mm stroke, in the upper third of the travel'
gazzew-boba-u4t-deep-dive.mdx § 'The bump: why it sits where it does' — 'the U4T's spread is only 6 grams'
